behaviorsAudited: constructs a child session from parent context, fork history, role config, and worktree context
runs the child provider turn loop through the same tool dispatch and rollout path as a normal session
forwards child messages, tool calls, tool results, usage, completion, and error events to the parent mailbox
drains parent-to-child trigger-turn messages between keep-alive turns without losing ordering
terminates cleanly on completion, provider error, max-turn exhaustion, mailbox close, or abort
admits root human input through exact opaque ownership tokens with bounded retained content and exact pre-consumption rollback

edgeCasesAudited: a follow-up trigger arriving while the child is idle starts the next turn on the same live child
a provider rejection records run_error and final errored status without leaving the child mailbox open
an abort while the provider stream is pending interrupts the child run and resolves the caller promise
silent mode suppresses parent notifications while preserving the canonical child rollout and final result
maxTurns equal to one reports a deterministic max-turn error after the first child assistant response
an input rejected before model consumption rolls back only its exact admission while consumed or indeterminate input is never duplicated

commandResults: PASS: 7 test files passed, 149 tests passed
implementationEvidence: runAgent builds child runtime context from parent services, fork messages, role policy, provider selection, worktree coordinates, and abort signals before entering the standard model turn path.

The child mailbox enforces count and UTF-8 byte bounds, protects trigger traffic, preserves FIFO prefix processing, emits bounded omission markers, and wakes waiters while salvaging admitted traffic on close.

runAgent appends each correlated terminal task outcome to the canonical child journal before any bounded, deduplicated parent projection; projection backpressure is explicit and cannot erase the durable outcome.
Silent mode suppresses parent relays only and preserves the canonical child rollout.
Session-owned idle-input admissions use opaque tokens over exact sequence identities, admit protected batches atomically, and roll back only records that remain unconsumed.
